assertions.expectation: Configures a recurring maintenance window for weekends only
assertions.reason: Both Terraform configuration and gcloud script configure Saturday 2-6 AM UTC weekly maintenance window using FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA recurrence

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start/end and --maintenance-window-recurrence for weekly weekend schedule
assertions.reason: The bash script includes gcloud command with --maintenance-window-start, --maintenance-window-end, and --maintenance-window-recurrence flags for Saturday schedule

assertions.expectation: Sets up maintenance exclusions for quarterly code freezes (up to 30 days each)
assertions.reason: Configuration includes Q1 (March 15-31), Q2 (June 15-30), Q3 (September 15-30) exclusions, each approximately 15-16 days, well under 30-day limit

assertions.expectation: Sets up a maintenance exclusion for the November audit period
assertions.reason: Configuration includes Q4 exclusion from November 1 - December 15 covering the November audit period

assertions.expectation: Notes the 30-day maximum for maintenance exclusions and how to handle longer freezes
assertions.reason: The response does not mention the 30-day maximum limitation for maintenance exclusions or provide guidance on handling longer freeze periods

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades bypass maintenance windows — only auto-upgrades respect them
assertions.reason: The response does not explain the distinction between manual and auto-upgrades or that maintenance windows only apply to auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends combining maintenance windows with Stable or Extended channel for maximum upgrade control
assertions.reason: The response uses REGULAR channel instead of recommending STABLE or EXTENDED channel for maximum control in regulated environments

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for each exclusion (--add-maintenance-exclusion-name/start-time/end-time)
assertions.reason: The bash script provides gcloud commands with --add-maintenance-exclusion-name, --add-maintenance-exclusion-start, --add-maintenance-exclusion-end flags for each exclusion period

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risk of falling too far behind on versions during long exclusion periods
assertions.reason: The response does not warn about version drift risks or the potential security and supportability issues of falling behind on GKE versions during extended exclusion periods
